prompt: How do I extend the lifetime of a blob on Walrus?
expected_output: Use walrus extend --blob-obj-id <ID>. The blob must not be expired. Use the Sui object ID, not the blob ID.
expectations: Shows the walrus extend command with --blob-obj-id
States that the blob must not be expired
Clarifies that the command takes the Sui object ID, not the blob ID
Mentions that shared blobs can also be extended with --shared flag

prompt: If I delete a blob on Walrus, is the data completely gone?
expected_output: No. Delete only removes slivers from current/future nodes. Other copies may exist. Encryption is the only reliable privacy mechanism.
expectations: States that delete does not guarantee complete data removal
Mentions that other copies of the same blob may exist if uploaded by others
Mentions caches and past storage nodes are not affected
Recommends encryption as the reliable privacy mechanism

prompt: What happens when a Walrus blob expires?
expected_output: Data becomes unavailable at the start of the end epoch. No grace period. Storage nodes discard slivers. Must re-upload to restore.
expectations: States that data becomes unavailable when the blob expires
Mentions there is no grace period
States that expired blobs cannot be extended
Mentions the need to re-upload if data must persist
